title: Chaetoseris bonatii
taxonomy: Chaetoseris bonatii is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family, Asteraceae. It is a perennial herb that grows to a height of around 10-50 cm. This plant is native to China and is often found in mountainous regions. The classification of Chaetoseris bonatii is that it belongs to the family Asteraceae, tribe Cichorieae, and genus Chaetoseris. Further classification includes it being a member of the species C. bonatii within the genus Chaetoseris.

intro: Chaetoseris bonatii, also known as Tree of Heaven or Ailanthus, is a deciduous tree native to Asia and widely naturalized in other parts of the world. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, fever, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Chaetoseris bonatii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
